product_overview: Viru Tracker is a Next.js frontend for tracking fares, watchlist items, alerts, and user preferences with a private dashboard and search capabilities. This PRD captures UI flows, core goals, and end-to-end happy/error paths to guide testing and implementation.
core_goals: Provide secure user authentication (register/login) and protect private routes
Allow users to create, view, edit, filter, and delete alert rules
Enable managing a watchlist with add/remove and manual refresh operations
Expose quick search with suggestions and preserve recent searches in history
Offer user preferences (display, notifications, API provider) with save/retrieve
Surface operational endpoints (health/readiness) and support testability for backend integration

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Allow users to register and log in using email/password, show validation and server error messages, and redirect authenticated users to the dashboard.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click login/register button -> See success confirmation or redirect to /login
Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click login/register button -> Redirect to /dashboard

Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field (wrong password) -> Click login/register button -> See error messages on invalid credentials -> Observe 401 response from POST /api/v1/auth/login

Navigate to /register -> Fill email input field with already-registered email -> Fill password input field -> Click login/register button -> See error messages on invalid credentials or conflict response from POST /api/v1/auth/register

features.name: Alert Management
features.description: Create, list, edit, delete and filter alert rules from the Alerts page for authenticated users.
user_flows: Navigate to /alerts -> View list of alerts -> Click Create new alert -> Fill alert form fields -> Click Save -> See new alert in list
Navigate to /alerts -> View list of alerts -> Click an alert to Edit existing alert -> Modify fields -> Click Save -> See updated alert in list
Navigate to /alerts -> Click Create new alert -> Leave required fields empty -> Click Save -> See error messages on invalid input
Navigate to /alerts -> View list of alerts -> Click Delete on an alert -> Confirm deletion -> See alert removed from list or observe DELETE /api/alerts/{id} 404 if id missing

features.name: Watchlist Management
features.description: List, add, remove and inspect watchlist items; allow manual refresh of an item snapshot.
user_flows: Navigate to /watchlist -> View watchlist items -> Click Add item -> Fill watchlist item fields -> Click Add -> See new item in list
Navigate to /watchlist -> View watchlist items -> Click on a watchlisted item -> View details of watchlisted item
Navigate to /watchlist -> View watchlist items -> Click Remove on an item -> Confirm removal -> See item removed from list
Navigate to /watchlist -> View watchlist items -> Click Refresh on an item -> See updated snapshot or success response from POST /api/v1/watchlist/{watch_id}/refresh-now
Navigate to /watchlist -> Click Add item -> Submit with missing required fields -> See error messages on invalid input

features.name: Search History
features.description: Show a list of past searches, let users re-run searches from history, delete items individually, or clear all history.
user_flows: Navigate to /history -> View search history list -> Click on history item to repeat search -> Redirect to /dashboard -> View search results
Navigate to /history -> View search history list -> Click Delete on a history item -> Confirm deletion -> See item removed from list or observe DELETE /api/history/{id}
Navigate to /history -> View search history list -> Click Clear all history -> Confirm clear -> See empty history list
Navigate to /history -> Click Delete on a history item that no longer exists -> See error message or observe 404 from DELETE /api/v1/history/{id}

features.name: User Preferences
features.description: Allow users to read and update display, notification and API provider preferences and persist changes to the backend.
user_flows: Navigate to /preferences -> View preferences -> Update display preferences -> Change notification settings -> Click Save -> See success confirmation and persisted values from GET/PUT /api/v1/preferences
Navigate to /preferences -> Update API provider preferences -> Click Save -> See changes applied in UI
Navigate to /preferences -> Make changes -> Click Save -> Observe validation error on invalid values
Navigate to /preferences -> Make changes -> Click Save -> See server error message on failed PUT /api/v1/preferences or network failure

features.name: Search and Suggestions (Quick Search)
features.description: Provide quick search on the dashboard with inline suggestions and ability to submit queries to view results.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Type in search field -> See search suggestions dropdown -> Click suggestion to select -> Submit search query -> View search results
Navigate to /dashboard -> Type in search field -> See search suggestions dropdown -> Use keyboard to select suggestion -> Press Enter to submit -> View search results
Navigate to /dashboard -> Type in search field -> Network or backend error occurs for suggestions -> See empty suggestions list or error indicator
Navigate to /dashboard -> Submit an empty search query -> See validation error and no request to /api/v1/search/quick

features.name: Operational Health & Readiness
features.description: Expose backend health and readiness endpoints to verify service availability and readiness for integration tests.
user_flows: Send GET request to /health -> Receive 200 OK -> Service reports healthy
Send GET request to /ready -> Receive 200 OK -> Service reports ready
Send GET request to /health -> Receive non-200 or timeout -> Report degraded or unavailable status

features.name: User Authentication
user_interactions: See error messages on invalid credentials

features.name: Search History
user_interactions: Click on history item to repeat search

known_limitations.issue: Frontend not connected to backend API
known_limitations.impact: All API calls may be mocked or not functional until backend is running
Users may lose session on page refresh if localStorage not implemented
